🎯 Management Attention

generated 2026-07-21 02:12 UTC · guidelines: docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md
Under control?No — 5 items need attention (2 escalate-now, 3 needs-management-attention)
Urgent vs smaller5 urgent, 7 need a nudge, 13 routine
Total open (active, excl. triage)25 +4 vs last digest (21 → 25)
2
🚨 Escalate now
3
🔴 Needs attention
7
🟡 Watch
13
🟢 Under control

🆕 New since last digest

  • INC-14929 — P3, jojobet slow processing, lead assigned, Monitoring within 35min, no breach
  • INC-14925 — P3, H2Bet rewards-casino-count-service lag, lead assigned, Monitoring within 78min, no breach
  • INC-14920 — P3, Tinbet email-distributor queue, lead assigned, accepted within 10min, no breach
  • INC-14864 — P2, ElastiCache memory/CPU, node upsized within 55min, kept open for cleanup scripts, no breach
  • INC-14863 — P2, Power Play GiG tag-callback 500s, partner comms took ~3h46m (slower than C9 bar), clean lead handoff, flagged for morning handover

✅ Resolved since last digest

  • INC-14847 — closed clean — Meridianbet 'the usual' alert cluster self-resolved within ~40min, no breach
Guideline codes legend (docs/incident-handling-guidelines.md)
  • A1 — Every incident has an owner and Head-of-SRE-level visibility
  • A2 — No incident is “just closed” — every problem gets an action
  • A3 — “It's just 10/30 minutes” is never an acceptable rationale
  • A4 — Recurring problems are escalated to the owning team for a long-term fix
  • B5 — Escalate early — not after hours without recovery
  • B6 — Security incidents are P1 until de-escalated
  • B7 — Critical-path failures are top priority
  • B8 — Night and handover incidents must not be lost — assign, don't just mention
  • C9 — Inform partners fast, even before root cause is known
  • C10 — Close the loop — recovery comms sent, partner questions answered
  • C11 — Comms accurate and crystal clear on impact
  • D12 — Informed decisions over passive waiting
  • D13 — Runbooks followed, basic checks done
  • D14 — Don't close while partner-side work remains open
  • D15 — The incident record is self-contained and phone-readable
  • E16 — Digest answers: under control? urgent vs smaller? new bugs? how many open?

🚨 Escalate now 2

INC-8064 slack intercomP1open 55d· evidence to 20 Jul 13:02 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : H2Bet Production
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: correction — H2Bet ACCEPTED the interim workaround (discussed in the meeting with them and our Partner Manager, early Jul); that acceptance is why REW-176 was opened on 9 Jul. The 28 May rejection was the earlier 503-as-success proposal only. No sign-off is pending from H2Bet.

All three breaches behind the 🚨 flag (A2/A4, B5, C10/D15) still hold with zero change this cycle. This reflects the 16 Jul management note (H2Bet accepted the workaround) but the note does not resolve the underlying breaches. Now ~55 days open.

H2Bet duplicate bonus grants confirmed as root cause within hours of the 26 May report, but ~55 days later there is still no deployed fix — roughly 300 duplicate grants/week continue. Per the management note, H2Bet has accepted the interim workaround and REW-176 remains parked on Rewards-team capacity until mid-August. Zero new activity anywhere again this cycle.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: still holds — ~55 days in, no decisive fix deployed, REW-176 still parked
  • B5: still holds — zero evidence of any BetConstruct response or outreach progress since 18 Jun
  • C10/D15: still holds — Intercom conversation remains closed while incident.io stays open P1 with no incident.io-side explanation of the closure or REW-176 status
Proposed / pending
  • REW-176 interim workaround - ACCEPTED by H2Bet (per management note), still blocked by Rewards capacity until mid-Aug — no movement observed
Timeline (1)
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack messages, no new Intercom parts, no incident.io update text — fully static again, ~56 days open

→ Force a decision point: get Rewards-team capacity committed to REW-176 with a hard date, or explicitly accept the ~300/week duplicate-grant cost until mid-August and say so in the record.

INC-14476 slack intercomP1open 3d· evidence to 20 Jul 07:04 UTC
[P1] Intercom Incident : Mystake Production

Still 🚨: True P1, partner silent ~76h (3.2 days) since the 17 Jul technical ask despite FT's strongest mitigation to date (recon service fully stopped) and repeated outreach — but the account-manager escalation suggested across 7 prior digests still has not happened, now an 8th cycle. B5's 'system clearly not recovering with no escalation in sight' still applies.

Cocobet (Mystake) blocked users appear to 'unblock' themselves because the partner's daily recon feed omits users who should stay blocked. Joachim sent the partner a clear technical ask on 17 Jul; the partner has been silent since. FT has applied progressively stronger mitigation (origin resync + blacklist, then stopping the recon service entirely) and made two fresh outreach attempts last cycle (Intercom + a direct DM from Ilke) — still no reply. Account-manager escalation still has not happened, now a 6th consecutive digest cycle flagging it.

Open breaches
  • B5: still no account-manager escalation despite 6 consecutive digests suggesting it — system not recovering, only stronger internal mitigation applied
  • C9/C10: partner silent ~74h (3.1 days) since the 17 Jul 10:17 UTC technical ask, despite three FT follow-up attempts (Intercom ×2 + direct DM)
  • D15: incident.io record still doesn't reflect the recon-service-stopped mitigation or the direct DM outreach
Proposed / pending
  • Resync affected origin via user migration and blacklist Cocobet's origin from the daily recon job — applied ~19:47 CEST 17 Jul
  • Stop Cocobet's recon service entirely — applied 06:52 CEST 20 Jul
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-17T08:42
  • root-cause explanation and recon-feed check request — delivered 12:18-12:19 CEST 17 Jul; no partner reply since, confirmed still silent through this run
Timeline (1)
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack/Intercom activity; partner (Sandro/Cocobet-side contact) still silent since 17 Jul technical ask — unchanged from last cycle

→ Escalate to Cocobet's account manager today — 8th consecutive digest flagging this; direct engineer-to-partner DMs and Intercom follow-ups are not a substitute for a formal account-management escalation on a 76h-silent True P1.

🔴 Needs attention 3

INC-11079 slackP2open 23d· evidence to 19 Jul 02:30 UTC
segurobet: no consumers on queue segurobet.consentsmanager (messages: 1, in: 0/s)
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: this is in reality a BetConstruct platform issue (their Operator API returns 500 on consent updates). The partner is aware and has raised it with BetConstruct themselves; active partner correspondence (examples provided, regular chasing incl. by Andrea) lives in the partner channel thread: https://fasttrack-solutions.slack.com/archives/C08VB1RBJLF/p1782549375502499 — future runs should treat that thread as the live track, not the quiet incident channel.

Management note (16 Jul) held this at watch-level as a BetConstruct-side blocker with active chasing, with escalation if unanswered much longer — that condition remains met, now more strongly: Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase has had zero reply for ~4.6 days on a P2 open ~24 days with no FT-side mitigation.

Segurobet Consent Manager remains stalled since 27 Jun on partner (BetConstruct) Operator API 500s. Per management note, the live track is the partner thread, not this incident channel. No reply there since Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) — now ~96h (4.0 days) unanswered, confirmed via a direct read of the partner thread this cycle.

Open breaches
  • C9/C10: BetConstruct has not responded to Andrea's 16 Jul 12:54 CEST chase in the #ftcrm-segurobet partner thread — now ~110h (4.6 days) of partner silence on an unresolved P2 open since 27 Jun (~24 days); no FT-side mitigation applied in the meantime
Proposed / pending
  • notify partner + supply failing examples - done
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-27T08:36
  • last partner message unanswered
  • 'we'll check' (01 Jul) - no ETA ever given; Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase remains unanswered
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 12:54 CEST: Andrea's re-chase to Eduardo Nunes (BetConstruct) — still unanswered
  • 19 Jul 04:30 CEST: James Hodgson left the incident channel
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): direct re-read of #ftcrm-segurobet confirms the newest message in the partner thread is still a 17 Jul 22:55 CEST deleted-message notice — no partner reply since Andrea's 16 Jul re-chase, now ~110h (4.6 days) of silence

→ Escalate directly with BetConstruct account management — now ~4.6 days of silence on Andrea's own re-chase, on a 24-day-old P2 with player consent updates still not applying in real time.

INC-14640 slackP2open 2d· evidence to 20 Jul 06:43 UTC
[FIRING:1] Rewards: GraphQL API errors (Grafana) Alerting (true rewards wemalaysia: TIMEOUT)

🔴 — two breaches: the 18 Jul commitment to raise the JWT-timing issue with Ryan Yu 'Monday' has now fully missed its window with no evidence it happened (A2/A4), and the Incident Lead left the channel that same evening with nobody reassigned (A1). Alert itself is quiet (self-resolved) but the follow-through has stalled.

Rewards GraphQL API timeout/JWT-auth errors for the wemalaysia brand. The underlying Grafana alert self-resolved overnight (19 Jul); root cause is wemalaysia's own frontend occasionally sending a malformed JWT before a valid one — known, recurring, not an FT bug. Team agreed 18 Jul to raise it with Ryan Yu 'Monday' (20 Jul) — that day has now fully passed with zero evidence the conversation happened, and lead James Hodgson left the incident channel that evening with no successor assigned.

Open breaches
  • A2/A4: agreed 'raise with Ryan Yu Monday' (18 Jul) — the Monday has now fully passed with zero evidence it happened
  • A1: Incident Lead (James Hodgson) left the channel 20 Jul 20:48 CEST with no successor assigned — owner visibility gap
Proposed / pending
  • Raise JWT-generation timing with Ryan Yu Monday — agreed, not yet actioned
Timeline (5)
  • 18 Jul 21:21 CEST status moved to Monitoring, 'will pick this up on monday'
  • 18 Jul 22:50 CEST lead handed to James Hodgson
  • 20 Jul (the Monday itself): zero Slack activity referencing Ryan Yu or the JWT-timing fix all day
  • 20 Jul 20:48 CEST James Hodgson (Incident Lead) leaves the channel — no successor assigned, no handover note
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): Monday has fully elapsed with the agreed action never actioned, and the lead has left the channel

→ Reassign an Incident Lead and get the Ryan Yu conversation actioned today — the agreed Monday commitment has now fully lapsed.

INC-10902 slackP3open 25d· evidence to 20 Jul 11:39 UTC
H2Bet Clickhouse Writer Service Investigation

Upgraded 🟡→🔴: this was fresh and forgivably unactioned last cycle (~1h old), but it has now sat overnight (~14.5h) with zero movement — no permission grant, no decision, no owner for the other 34 volumes — despite Patrik and five senior engineers being in the channel at the moment it was raised. B5/D12: a clear case of 'no escalation in sight' now that the grace period has fully elapsed.

Recurring H2Bet nightly ClickHouse-writer pileup. Matteo's 20 Jul 13:36 CEST finding (ClickHouse EBS gp3 throughput ceiling, ready one-command fix blocked only on an IAM permission gap, plus the same risk flagged across 35+ other volumes fleet-wide) drew Patrik Potocki and five senior engineers into the channel the same moment — but there has now been zero follow-up for ~14.5h overnight: no permission grant, no decision, no owner assigned to the other 34 volumes.

Open breaches
  • D12: a ready, cheap ($15/mo), one-command fix for a 14+ day recurring crash-loop has sat blocked on an IAM permission for ~14.5h overnight, with no decision recorded, despite Patrik and five senior engineers joining the channel
  • A4: the fleet-wide risk (35+ ClickHouse volumes sharing the same default-throughput exposure) still has no assigned owner
Proposed / pending
  • Schema/type-mismatch fix (ActionEvent.Status int vs string) — still not implemented, ownership handed to Matteo as of 17 Jul
Timeline (3)
  • 20 Jul 13:36 CEST Matteo posts the gp3-throughput root cause, a ready one-command fix (blocked on ec2:ModifyVolume permission), and flags 35+ other at-risk volumes fleet-wide
  • 20 Jul 13:36 CEST Patrik Potocki, Stephen Riolo, Max Holdaway, Agustín Castaño, Curt Healey and Andrew Pirotta all join the channel
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero Slack activity since — ~14.5h overnight with no permission grant, no decision, and no owner assigned to review the other volumes, despite six senior people having joined the channel at the moment it was flagged

→ First thing this morning: grant Matteo (or have someone with access run) the ec2:ModifyVolume fix for h2bet, and assign an owner to review the other 34 flagged ClickHouse volumes — six senior people already saw this and nothing has moved overnight.

🟡 Watch 7

INC-12022 slack intercomP1open 18d· evidence to 20 Jul 08:32 UTC
Shortlinks Redirecting to "Forbidden" page
📝 Management note (2026-07-16) Andrea: Solved as an incident. Only pending item is with Estelarbet, who need to identify which players were impacted — this is being checked and shared from our side; we need their input on which players complained so we can cross-check. Confirmed via the partner Intercom ticket.

Downgraded 🔴→🟡: the flagged C9/C10 breach (partner scope-clarification unanswered ~64h) is now resolved — Giancarlo replied 20 Jul 14:57 UTC — but the reply itself came very late (64h, well past same-day) and made a fresh promise ('update tomorrow', due 21 Jul) that needs checking next run.

Cloudflare-origin check blocked shortlink/unsubscribe traffic; mitigated same day, fix merged 02 Jul. Per Andrea's management note this is solved as an incident, pending only Estelarbet's cross-check. Partner (Gloria Morocho) clarified scope on 17 Jul (all urls affected, not just unsubscribe) and got no FT reply for ~64h; Giancarlo finally replied 20 Jul 14:57 UTC saying he regrouped with the partner manager and will give a further update 'tomorrow' (21 Jul) — partner acknowledged positively. That fresh promise is now the item to verify next run.

Proposed / pending
  • CF-origin carve-out - merged 02 Jul
  • deliver full partner report - cross-check inconclusive, now needs to incorporate the partner's broader-scope clarification
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-02T13:29
  • last partner message unanswered
  • RCA/report promised repeatedly (13-15 Jul), still not fully delivered
Timeline (3)
  • 17 Jul 22:35 UTC partner (Gloria Morocho) clarifies scope: all urls affected, not only unsubscribe
  • 20 Jul 14:57 UTC (this run) Giancarlo replies on Intercom — ~64h after the clarification — says he regrouped with the partner manager, promises an update 'tomorrow' (21 Jul); Gloria acknowledges positively 14:58 UTC
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): partner reply landed last cycle closing the ~64h silence gap; new promise ('update tomorrow') not yet due

→ Confirm Giancarlo's promised 21 Jul update to Gloria Morocho actually goes out.

INC-10045 slack intercomP2open 32d· evidence to 20 Jul 11:47 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Power Play Production

Continued thorough, collaborative investigation with Integration/PM engaged, but the internal decision (scoping answer / what to tell the partner) has now been pending since Friday (~6 days) despite Matteo's fresh push; the Intercom SLA remains formally missed.

GiG (Power Play's upstream KYC) event-ordering bug leaves stale block tags. The scoping answer Robert asked Stephen/Curt for on 17 Jul is still outstanding; Matteo has now directly nudged Stephen and Robert for a decision so the partner can be told what to expect and the ticket handed to Lis — the internal decision point has been open since Friday (3 days) with no resolution yet.

Proposed / pending
  • update_epoch as ordering key — Robert asked Stephen/Curt to scope size of this fallback vs pushing GiG to fix event ordering themselves; scoping answer still pending
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-16T12:16
  • notify once re-syncs complete - kept
  • structural handover to Integration/PM drafted 16 Jul, sent to Robert/Lis/Gui 17 Jul
Timeline (2)
  • 20 Jul 13:47 CEST Matteo nudges Stephen Riolo and Robert Friberg for a decision on what was discussed Friday, wants to finalize what to tell the partner, and hand over to Lis
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack/Intercom activity; Matteo's 13:47 CEST nudge to Stephen/Robert still unanswered, now ~14h old; internal decision pending since Friday (now ~6 days)

→ Stephen/Robert to give Matteo a same-day answer so partner comms and the handover to Lis can proceed — decision has been pending since Friday, now nearly a week.

INC-14346 slackP2open 4d· evidence to 17 Jul 21:42 UTC
CF Threat Watch: brute-force-suspect,login-success-after-fails on ft-crm.com (41.76.35.208)

Handling was fast and correct (B6 escalation, false-positive determination), but the record has now sat unclosed ~4.6 days since the conclusion settled — still a minor D15 record-hygiene gap, not a live incident concern.

CF Threat Watch brute-force-suspect alert on ft-crm.com. Correct B6 security escalation flow despite paging misfires; team read is a shared-network false positive per Cloudflare's own classification. Status remains 'Investigating' with no incident.io updates since 2026-07-16T12:40:33Z; incident.io's own auto-nudge on 17 Jul asking to mark it resolved remains unactioned, now ~4.3 days since the false-positive conclusion settled.

Open breaches
  • D15: incident sitting in 'Investigating' ~4.3 days with the conclusion settled (shared-network false positive) and the automated resolve-nudge still unactioned
Timeline (3)
  • 16 Jul 12:40 — last incident.io status update; team settles on shared-network false-positive read
  • 17 Jul 23:42 CEST — incident.io bot auto-prompts to mark resolved — still no reply
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack activity; resolve-nudge from 17 Jul still unactioned, now ~4.6 days since the false-positive conclusion settled

→ Stephen Riolo to click resolve/close now that the false-positive conclusion is settled.

INC-14863 slackP2open 11h· evidence to 20 Jul 22:39 UTC
Power Play: no consumers on queue power-play.actions.callbackconsumer (messages: 69881, in: 0/s)

🟡 — one moderate breach: partner comms slower than guideline (~3h46m) despite otherwise good mechanics (immediate ack, clean lead handoff with a written status, correctly flagged for morning handover). Worth checking next run whether the partner has replied.

Power Play's GiG-operator tag-action callback consumer started failing (500s: tag-ID type/format mismatches on assign/unassign calls), queue backing up. Gérard acked immediately but spent ~3h45m determining whether this was the same root cause as the existing INC-10045 GiG-tags incident before sending partner-facing comms; team flagged it for morning standup discussion. Lead handed cleanly from Gérard → James Hodgson at shift end; James's late-night update (~22:26 UTC) notes the issue is still ongoing with no partner reply yet.

Open breaches
  • C9: partner-facing comms took ~3h46m from alert to send (20:35 CEST vs 16:49 CEST) — slower than the C9 bar (~40min-2h), though much of the delay was legitimate internal disambiguation against INC-10045, not neglect
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-20T14:49
  • comms sent to partner-facing channel 20:35 CEST — no reply yet
Timeline (7)
  • 20 Jul 14:49 UTC (16:49 CEST) alert fires (2 alerts), Gérard Pelayo acks and is set lead
  • 20 Jul 16:59-17:00 CEST Gérard finds gig-operator-api errors: tag-ID type mismatches (Guid vs int) on assign/unassign calls
  • 20 Jul 20:00-20:47 CEST team (Gérard, Simon Brown, Giancarlo) debates whether this is the same issue as INC-10045 (GiG tag ordering) — inconclusive; Simon asks to add to handover for standup
  • 20 Jul 20:35 CEST Gérard sends partner-facing comms (~3h46m after the alert)
  • 20 Jul 21:40 CEST status moved to Waiting on Partner
  • 20 Jul 23:06 CEST James Hodgson takes over as lead from Gérard (shift handoff)
  • 21 Jul 00:29 CEST (22:26 UTC) James posts: issue still ongoing, no partner reply yet, with fresh 500 error detail

→ Confirm with Matteo in the morning (per team's own plan) whether this is the same root cause as INC-10045, and check for partner reply.

frank-casino ping-pong deployment stuck in CrashLoopBackOff (403 from integration API on ping). No new activity this cycle. Per management note, the deployment ping-pong service is not critical (B7 does not apply).

INC-14331 slackP3open 4d· evidence to 20 Jul 15:27 UTC
Leonbets Activity Manager Groups Alerts Spamming

🟡 — real progress (Malcolm actively reviewing, substantive comments left), but the durable merge still hasn't landed and kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on the next OneClick deploy.

Leonbets activity-manager DB-saturation alert spam; Matteo's scoped-loading fix (PR #490) proven effective over 4+ days in production on Leonbets (alerts stopped, lag under ~10k). Malcolm Camilleri (back from leave) is now actively reviewing PR #490 and confirmed the maxReplicas tuning; review in progress but not yet merged.

Open breaches
  • A2: durable fix merge (PR #490) still unconfirmed; kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on next OneClick deploy. Now over 5 days without a human update confirming merge status.
Proposed / pending
  • Scoped action loading (PR #490) — proven effective, merge status still unconfirmed; durable persistence of env vars still pending
Timeline (3)
  • 20 Jul 13:51-13:53 CEST Matteo confirms image running clean on Leonbets since Friday, asks Malcolm to review PR #490
  • 20 Jul 17:27 CEST Malcolm Camilleri leaves PR review comments (readability + a further loaded-actionGroups improvement idea) — approval/merge still pending
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): PR #490 still not merged; kubectl-level mitigation remains revert-exposed on next OneClick deploy

→ Malcolm Camilleri to complete review/merge PR #490 this week so the fix becomes durable ahead of the next OneClick deploy.

🟡 — the ~30h B5 staleness is now moot since the lag has cleared, but the incident record hasn't been closed/updated yet to reflect that; P3, judged leniently, check next run that it gets closed.

Kafka Forwarder Lag (benko-igp-azur) alert fired 19 Jul 17:04 UTC, accepted into Investigating 21:49 UTC same day, then sat with no update/activity for ~30h. The attached alert auto-resolved 21 Jul 02:29 CEST (just before this run) — the lag has cleared — but the incident.io status hasn't been updated to reflect it yet.

Open breaches
  • D15/C10: alert cleared 02:29 CEST but incident status still shows Investigating — not yet a breach (~1.5h old), but worth closing out next run if still open

13× routine/clean: jojobet & H2Bet & Tinbet slow-processing (NEW, all quiet), ElastiCache memory (NEW, fixed), LeonBets permission bug (Waiting on Partner confirm), Kyngs WAF false-positive (fixed, awaiting retry), Betconstruct cron nodes (P4 internal), Pokerklas deposit gap (P1, FT chasing, not yet a breach), Sub-agent dashboard-admin (P4, downgraded, fixed), CVE-2021-45046 (Patrik-deferred), Stanleybet Romania (fix deployed, awaiting verify), Calendars 12play door-variation (TH/SG rollout continuing), 12play/we88 fire-late (RabbitMQ cutover ongoing, quiet).

Email security scanners triggered false unsubscribe signals for Stanleybet Romania (no emails actually suppressed). Fix (PR #106) deployed this morning; Giancarlo asked the partner to verify on Intercom and is chasing PR reviews.

Proposed / pending
  • Fix in review (conversion-service-go PR #106), deploy/test Monday - agreed with partner

  • first reported 2026-07-16T12:41
  • test fix Monday — agreed with partner

  • 20 Jul 08:29 UTC Giancarlo asks partner (Marius) on Intercom to verify unsub metrics on next activities

INC-14845 slack intercomP2open 13h· evidence to 20 Jul 14:37 UTC
[P2] Intercom Incident : Kyngs Production

No breach — P2 handled fast and thoroughly (~1h42m to deployed fix), with proper security-change review discipline maintained even under time pressure and even though the approver was on vacation. Textbook handling.

Cloudflare WAF rule (attack-ML scoring) was blocking the calculated-segment-field save endpoint (AggCalculatedFields/Upsert) on Kyngs Production with 403s — the same recurring false-positive class as three prior documented incidents (trigger expressions, action fields, unsubscribe HTML). Matteo Rapisarda root-caused it fast and, because it touched a security WAF rule, deliberately looped in Patrik Potocki and Stephen Riolo for review despite Patrik being on vacation; Patrik responded within minutes, Carl Bondin gave a second approval, PR #19 merged and deployed via Spacelift — reporters asked to retry. ~1h42m from alert to fix deployed.

Proposed / pending
  • PR #19: exclude AggCalculatedFields/Upsert from WAF attack-block rule — merged and deployed 16:22-16:37 CEST
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-20T12:55
  • asked reporters (Kyngs CRM users) to retry saving — pending confirmation
Timeline (6)
  • 20 Jul 12:55 UTC (14:55 CEST) alert fires, Matteo acknowledges and takes lead
  • 20 Jul 15:18-15:33 CEST Matteo identifies the WAF rule (ref ea2a7c8f) and root-causes the missing Upsert exclusion, flags honeypot auto-ban escalation risk, deliberately asks for Patrik/Stephen review given the security-sensitive change
  • 20 Jul 15:33-15:43 CEST Patrik Potocki (on vacation) and Stephen Riolo respond within minutes; Patrik authorises the change
  • 20 Jul 15:55-16:22 CEST PR #19 opened, reviewed (Carl Bondin approval), merged
  • 20 Jul 16:35-16:38 CEST Spacelift plan deployed, status moved to Waiting on Partner, reporters asked to retry
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack; Intercom shows Matteo confirmed fix + asked partner (cristian.popovici) to retry by 14:37 UTC, promised same-day ETA and delivered — partner engaged positively ('Sounds amazing!'), no reply yet confirming the retry worked
INC-14864 slackP2open 11h· evidence to 20 Jul 22:14 UTC
[FIRING:1] ElastiCache Memory Utilized alert Alerting production-202-gen (true p2 aws-elasticache)

No breach — fast ack and mitigation (~55min to clear both alerts), kept open honestly while cleanup finishes rather than closing prematurely; purely internal infra, no partner impact. Root cause of the memory growth is still open — worth a check next run that it doesn't recur.

Production Redis (ElastiCache, production-202-gen) hit high memory utilization and engine CPU. Gérard Pelayo acked immediately, Simon Brown took lead within ~45min and upsized the node (cache.r6g.4xlarge → 8xlarge), clearing both attached alerts within ~55min of the alert. Kept open (Monitoring) deliberately while cleanup scripts remove large keysets per-tenant (sbtsolutions, star-casino, stanleybet-romania) to establish a stable baseline; root cause of the memory growth itself still not identified.

Proposed / pending
  • Upsize ElastiCache node (applied 17:47 CEST) — effective immediately
  • Per-tenant keyset cleanup scripts — in progress
Timeline (5)
  • 20 Jul 14:54 UTC (16:54 CEST) alert fires, Gérard Pelayo acks immediately
  • 20 Jul 17:41 CEST Simon Brown takes over lead, moves to Investigating
  • 20 Jul 17:47-17:48 CEST node upsized to cache.r6g.8xlarge, both alerts resolved (green), status moved to Monitoring
  • 20 Jul 19:49-22:14 CEST Gérard runs per-tenant keyset cleanup scripts (sbtsolutions, star-casino, stanleybet-romania) to reduce steady-state memory
  • 20 Jul 20:50 CEST incident-bot status update posted summarizing mitigation + ongoing cleanup
INC-10810 slackP3open 25d· evidence to 17 Jul 20:02 UTC
12play brands fire late

No breach — fix already landed, status honestly reflects 'Fixing' (D14), formal ownership in place, P3 judged leniently. Quiet again this cycle.

12play/we88 (Momentum) players received rewards 45-50min late due to RDS overload; a real fix landed and status has held at 'Fixing' since 10 Jul while monitoring and planning a RabbitMQ 4.x cutover. Sam Lundborg holds Incident Lead (reassigned 17 Jul). No new activity this cycle.

Proposed / pending
  • RabbitMQ 4.x cutover coordination owned by Sam Lundborg — no update this cycle
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-06-24T16:00
  • update 12play via Intercom on fix progress - discussed internally, completion not confirmed
Timeline (1)
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new activity this cycle
INC-14423 slackP3open 4d· evidence to 20 Jul 06:59 UTC
Container using an image vulnerable to CVE-2021-45046

No breach — Patrik already reviewed and deferred this to next week; no new activity or regression this cycle.

Log4j CVE-2021-45046 found on a staging container. Patrik Potocki personally directed Agustín to ack and a Linear ticket to be opened so it can be tackled next week — an explicit, informed deprioritization. No new activity this cycle.

Proposed / pending
  • Upgrade log4j-core - in progress
  • Linear ticket for next-week fix - per Patrik's direction
Timeline (2)
  • Patrik Potocki is a participant (already reviewed/deferred to next week per prior green_note)
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack activity; Patrik's next-week deferral still holds, no regression
INC-14567 slackP3open 3d· evidence to 20 Jul 12:56 UTC
Sub agent potential issue: dashboard-admin agent type not allowed

No breach — root cause fully confirmed with a clear fix plan, scope narrowed to a non-production preview build, severity honestly downgraded to P4 same session. Prior B5 staleness flag is resolved by this cycle's activity.

llm-agent service rejected a dashboard-admin sub-agent invocation (allowlist restriction), affecting only a PR-preview build (PR #467) on brand rhino — no production/customer impact. Jeremy Curmi confirmed the full root cause this cycle (invalid subagent_type not caught as a runtime error, unlike schema errors) and proposed a durable fix; incident downgraded P3→P4 and moved Investigating→Fixing the same session.

Timeline (3)
  • 20 Jul 14:47 CEST Jeremy Curmi posts full root-cause writeup: PR #467 preview build only, no customer impact, proposes durable fix (non-fatal invalid-subagent_type handling) plus two mitigations
  • 20 Jul 14:55 CEST severity downgraded P3→P4, status Investigating→Fixing; Jeremy notes 'not something happening on all clients, still testing' / 'not really an incident'
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack activity; downgrade to P4/Fixing from last cycle still holds, no regression
INC-14855 slack intercomP3open 12h· evidence to 20 Jul 14:39 UTC
[P3] Intercom Incident : LeonBets

No breach — fast, correct root-cause diagnosis and fix within ~1 hour, no need to touch the underlying feature flag; awaiting routine partner confirmation.

LeonBets: one user (Petr) couldn't create a new segment (missing filter dropdown, 403 in browser console) after a new Unleash flag (crm-api-enforce-endpoint-permissions) started enforcing endpoint-level permissions on GET /ActivityManager/singularity/features. Matteo Rapisarda root-caused it to a missing Singularity:Features:View permission on Petr's account, granted it directly, and asked him to confirm — all within ~1 hour of the alert.

Proposed / pending
  • Grant Singularity:Features:View to Petr's account — applied 16:33 CEST
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-20T13:33
  • asked Petr to confirm UI works again — pending confirmation
Timeline (4)
  • 20 Jul 13:33 UTC (15:33 CEST) alert fires, escalation to L2 expired, Matteo self-acknowledges and takes lead
  • 20 Jul 16:18-16:27 CEST Matteo diagnoses: GET .../singularity/features requires Singularity:Features:View, which Petr's role lacks; the new Unleash flag now enforces this at the endpoint
  • 20 Jul 16:33-16:34 CEST Matteo grants the permission directly, updates incident.io, moves status to Waiting on Partner
  • 20 Jul 16:39 CEST channel renamed to reflect final incident name — no substantive change

Tinbet: queue tinbet.actions.emaildistributor is processing slow (messages: 5937, in: 0/s, delivery: 0/s)

No breach on metadata — lead assigned, accepted within 10 minutes.

Slow-processing alert, Tinbet email-distributor queue. Lead assigned (James Hodgson), accepted within ~10min, moved to Waiting on Partner. No P1/P2, no security signal, no staleness — metadata-only, no deep read this cycle.

Slow-processing alert, H2Bet rewards-casino-count-service lag. Lead assigned (James Hodgson), status Monitoring within ~78min. No P1/P2, no security signal, no staleness — metadata-only, no deep read this cycle.

jojobet: topic jojobet.integration is processing slow (lag: 88852) group: crm-integration-consumer-jojobet

No breach on metadata — lead assigned immediately, status progressed to Monitoring within 35 minutes.

Slow-processing alert, jojobet integration-consumer lag. Lead assigned (James Hodgson) same time as report, status already Monitoring within ~35min. No P1/P2, no security signal, no staleness — metadata-only, no deep read this cycle.

INC-13889 slack intercomP4open 6d· evidence to 17 Jul 20:01 UTC
Calendars: Orphan (but live) door variations in the database(!)

No breach — fix confirmed on the primary instance, partner kept informed at each step, remaining-instance rollout scoped with a timeframe; every partner message answered within minutes throughout.

Door-only Chinese variation bug on 12play's Promotional Calendar 3 (no player impact) — fix confirmed working on the 12MY instance by the partner (Sook); other instances (TH, SG) still show the issue, and Rewards Development is rolling the fix out to them over the coming days.

Proposed / pending
  • backoffice-v2 PR — shipped
  • rewards-backend PR — shipped
  • Roll out fix to 12play TH/SG instances — scoped, ETA 'coming days'
Partner view
  • first reported 2026-07-10T04:15
  • fix confirmed tested, deployed to 12play production — verified by partner 20 Jul
  • roll out to 12play TH/SG in the coming days — communicated 20 Jul
Timeline (2)
  • 20 Jul 10:11-10:33 UTC Sook flags TH/SG still affected; Rewards Development confirms rollout 'in the coming days'
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): zero new Slack/Intercom activity; TH/SG rollout 'in the coming days' still holds, no regression
INC-14830 slackP4open 20h· evidence to 20 Jul 15:56 UTC
Betconstruct cron job nodes

No breach — root cause found and fix proposed same morning; still not applied ~5h later but P4, internal-only, no partner impact — staleness alone isn't a breach here.

P4: LLM MCP scheduled-job cron pods failed to spawn on Betconstruct cluster nodes over the weekend. Giancarlo flagged it to Agustín/Andrew this morning; Andrew root-caused it to node DiskPressure and proposed either recycling the nodes or applying Agustín's fix (increase disk to 40GB). No partner impact — internal AI-team tooling.

Proposed / pending
  • Recycle affected nodes, or apply Agustín's fix (increase node disk to 40GB) — proposed 09:28 CEST 20 Jul, not yet applied
Timeline (2)
  • 20 Jul 09:28 CEST Andrew Pirotta root-causes it to DiskPressure on Betconstruct cluster nodes, proposes recycling nodes or applying Agustín's 40GB-disk fix
  • 21 Jul run (04:03 CEST nightly): only a channel-join (Simon Brown); fix still not applied, ~18.5h since proposed — still no breach, P4 internal-only, no partner impact
